accepted_residuals: Profiles are static JSON export rows, not a runtime database or dashboard.
Current rows are platform_account subjects; creator_record subjects remain absent until promoted cross-platform linkage exists.
Aggregate views are admitted-pool or selected-grid rollups, not full-channel or all-content creator averages.
Engagement inputs remain platform/source limited; missing inputs stay explicit missingness rather than zero-filled metrics.

current_metric_rollups.content_kind_inclusion_rule: youtube Shorts admitted into the fragrance transcript-bearing source pool only
limitations: Rollup covers the admitted fragrance Shorts pool only; it is not a channel-wide average.
Metrics are recaptured from live YouTube watch packets and are capture-time observations that may change after capture.
Engagement rate is unavailable because no admitted Short exposed view, like, and total-comment counts together in the live watch packets.
Cross-platform rollups are not authorized without promoted public-handle linkage evidence.
Average/median views are computed over 5 admitted Shorts (10 metric observations in total); sample adequacy is stronger_admitted_pool_n_8_plus and the value is not a representative creator average.
The admitted pool is fragrance and transcript-bearing, so selection can bias view averages relative to the creator's full Shorts or channel output.

average_comment_count.posture_reason_or_none: no admitted Short exposed a source-visible total_comment_count in the live watch packets

engagement_rate.posture_reason_or_none: no admitted Short exposed view, like, and total-comment counts together in the live watch packets

limitations: Profile is account-scoped to one youtube platform account; it is not a linked creator_record.
Metric rollup covers the admitted/selected source pool only; it is not a channel-wide average.
Engagement rate, average likes, and average total comments are unavailable until source-backed numerator fields exist.
Ideal/content-fit audience profile is not joined in this static view.
Cross-platform aggregate influence is blocked until promoted public-handle linkage evidence exists.
Average/median view rollups are directional admitted-pool statistics; sample_support must be shown or used to downgrade thin rows before influence-summary presentation.

Cross-platform rollups are not authorized without promoted public-handle linkage evidence.
Average/median views are computed over 5 admitted Shorts (10 metric observations in total); sample adequacy is stronger_admitted_pool_n_8_plus and the value is not a representative creator average.
The admitted pool is fragrance and transcript-bearing, so selection can bias view averages relative to the creator's full Shorts or channel output.
